description: RFC #531 Phase 3 — add an `oh update` subcommand that upgrades ONLY the .oh/ control plane of an OpenHarness-equipped repo from a newer source (`--from <dir>`), leaving every project-owned file untouched. `oh update` is the sibling of `oh init`: init seeds PROJECT files from .oh/templates/; update refreshes the .oh/ INFRASTRUCTURE itself — disjoint targets, so this slice does NOT depend on the still-open #334. The safety invariant — overlay writes ONLY under <target>/.oh/ (path-escape guard) — makes 'project source remains untouched' hold by construction. Version gate reuses .oh/cli/package.json#version (no new VERSION file). Self-contained pure-function pattern (no init.ts on this base to mirror): a pure runUpdate(opts, io) + exported assertDestInTarget guard in .oh/cli/src/commands/update.ts + argv parsing in cli.ts. Source = `--from <dir>` only (remote-fetch deferred, same precedent as init's deferred bundling). Five stories own DISJOINT files and run as one EDIT-only /delegate wave; the Advisor integrates, runs the gates (node build.mjs, tsc --noEmit, full vitest, eval-probe suite, grep sweep), and commits per story. Base is origin/development (standalone, slice-1/slice-2 pattern); the only #334 overlap is a one-line cli.ts subcommand-registration union.

userStories.title: Implement runUpdate core in .oh/cli/src/commands/update.ts
userStories.description: As OpenHarness, I need a pure, testable `runUpdate` that overlays a newer .oh/ control plane onto an equipped repo while structurally guaranteeing project source is never touched.
acceptanceCriteria: Create NEW file .oh/cli/src/commands/update.ts. Export `interface UpdateIO { stdout: (s: string) => void; stderr: (s: string) => void; }` and `interface UpdateOptions { targetDir: string; fromDir: string; force?: boolean; dryRun?: boolean; }` and `export async function runUpdate(opts: UpdateOptions, io: UpdateIO): Promise<number>`. PURE function: it MUST NOT call process.exit, read process.argv, or read process.cwd() (the caller resolves targetDir). Use node:fs (existsSync, statSync, lstatSync, mkdirSync, readdirSync, readFileSync, copyFileSync) and node:path only — there is NO commands/init.ts on this base to mirror, so the module is fully SELF-CONTAINED. Implement a local `walkFiles(root: string, dir: string, acc: string[]): void` that recurses with readdirSync({ withFileTypes: true }); for each entry call lstatSync and SKIP symlinks (both file and dir symlinks — never follow them, so a source .oh/ symlink can never pull external content into the target); recurse into real directories; for real files push `path.relative(root, abs).split(path.sep).join('/')` (POSIX relpath). EVERY string passed to io.stdout / io.stderr MUST end with a trailing newline '\n' (process.stdout.write appends none).

Preconditions (each returns 1 after writing a one-line message to io.stderr): (a) resolve fromOh = path.resolve(fromDir, '.oh'); if !existsSync(fromOh) || !statSync(fromOh).isDirectory() -> 'oh update: update source not found at <fromOh>. Pass --from <built-OpenHarness-checkout>; remote-fetch is deferred (#531).'; (b) resolve targetOh = path.resolve(targetDir, '.oh'); if !existsSync(targetOh) || !statSync(targetOh).isDirectory() -> 'oh update: not an OpenHarness-equipped repo (no .oh/ at <targetDir>). Run `oh init` / vendor .oh/ first.'; (c) if fromOh === targetOh -> 'oh update: source and target are the same .oh; nothing to update.'

Version gate: read the `version` string from <fromOh>/cli/package.json (available) and <targetOh>/cli/package.json (current) via JSON.parse(readFileSync). If either package.json is missing/unparseable/has no string version, treat that side's version as '0.0.0'. Compare with a ROBUST numeric dotted compare: split on '.', pad to 3 segments; for EACH segment take the substring before the first '-' or '+' (`seg.split(/[-+]/)[0]`) then parseInt(...,10), mapping NaN/empty to 0. This pre-release/build-suffix strip is REQUIRED: cli.ts's own fallback is '0.0.0-dev' and parseInt('0-dev') is 0, so a naive parse would make '0.1.0-dev' falsely EQUAL '0.1.0'. Compare segment-by-segment. Behavior: available>current -> proceed (report `updating .oh: <current> -> <available>`); available===current -> if !force report 'oh update: already up to date (v<current>)' and return 0 (no writes), else proceed and report re-overlay; available<current -> if !force write to io.stderr 'oh update: refusing downgrade (current v<current> > source v<available>); pass --force to override' and return 1, else proceed (report downgrade).

Overlay: walk FILE relpaths under fromOh; for each relpath R: SKIP if ANY path SEGMENT of R (R.split('/'), so a NESTED 'cli/node_modules/pkg/x' is caught, not just a top-level one) equals 'node_modules' or 'dist' (volatile/build artifacts) — always emit the `skip <R> (volatile)` report line. Compute dest = path.resolve(targetOh, R) and call the EXPORTED guard `assertDestInTarget(dest, targetOh, path.sep)` (see next AC) BEFORE any write. If existsSync(dest) report `overwrite <R>` else `create <R>`; when NOT dryRun: mkdirSync(path.dirname(dest), { recursive: true }) then copyFileSync(src, dest). Under dryRun, prefix every report line with '[dry-run] ' and perform NO filesystem writes (no mkdir, no copy) — this dryRun write-suppression MUST hold EVEN when --force is set (a force-downgrade --dry-run still writes nothing).

Export the pure guard `export function assertDestInTarget(dest: string, targetOh: string, sep: string): void` that throws `new Error('oh update: refusing to write outside target .oh: ' + dest)` UNLESS (dest === targetOh || dest.startsWith(targetOh + sep)); runUpdate calls it for every dest. Extracting it as a named export is REQUIRED so US-003 can unit-test the safety invariant directly (a filesystem fixture cannot trigger it — path.relative never yields a '..'-prefixed relpath from a real entry).

Determinism + return: sort relpaths before iterating. On a successful overlay return 0. Emit EXACTLY ONE final summary line to io.stdout in this EXACT format (so US-003 asserts it deterministically): `oh update: <x> created, <y> overwritten, <s> skipped\n` (under dryRun prefix the whole line with '[dry-run] '; counts reflect planned actions). All normal output goes to io.stdout; only precondition/refusal errors go to io.stderr.
Owns ONLY .oh/cli/src/commands/update.ts (new). Does NOT edit cli.ts, the test file, probes, RESULTS.md, README, or CHANGELOG.
userStories.notes: Pin the runUpdate/UpdateOptions/UpdateIO signature EXACTLY as written — US-002 imports against it. Do not create .oh/cli/src/commands/init.ts (that is PR #334's file).

userStories.title: Wire `oh update` into cli.ts dispatch + help
userStories.description: As an operator, I need `oh update` reachable from the CLI with flag parsing and help, without disturbing the existing config/help/version behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit .oh/cli/src/cli.ts ONLY. Add `import { runUpdate } from "./commands/update.js";` (NodeNext .js specifier resolving to commands/update.ts — matches the repo's existing import style). Do NOT add or import anything from ./commands/init.js (that file does not exist on this base; it is PR #334's).

In main(argv), add a branch `if (first === "update") { ... }` BEFORE the final unknown-command fallthrough and adjacent to where a sibling subcommand would sit. Parse the remaining argv: support `--from <dir>` (value-taking; missing value -> stderr 'oh update: --from requires a directory' + return 1), boolean `--dry-run` and `--force`, and `--help`/`-h` (-> printUpdateHelp(); return 0). Unknown flags/positionals -> stderr `oh update: unexpected argument "<arg>"` + printUpdateHelp() + return 1. If --from is absent after parsing -> stderr 'oh update: --from <dir> is required (remote-fetch deferred, #531)' + return 1.

Resolve targetDir = process.cwd() (the equipped repo root; cli.ts is the ONLY place cwd is read — keep runUpdate pure). Call `return await runUpdate({ targetDir, fromDir, force, dryRun }, { stdout: (s) => process.stdout.write(s), stderr: (s) => process.stderr.write(s) });`.

Add a `printUpdateHelp()` function (mirroring printConfigHelp's style) describing: usage `oh update --from <dir> [--dry-run] [--force]`, that it upgrades ONLY the .oh/ control plane (project source untouched), --from is a built OpenHarness checkout (remote-fetch deferred), --dry-run previews, --force overrides the up-to-date/downgrade gate. Add an `update  Upgrade the .oh/ control plane from a newer source` line to printOhHelp's command list (a new 'Commands:' grouping is fine, or extend the Usage block) so `oh --help` advertises it.

Do NOT change isHelpFlag/isVersionFlag, the INTEGRATIONS framework, the `config` branch, the version flag, or the top-level main().then(process.exit) wiring. `node .oh/cli/build.mjs` must bundle clean and `cd .oh/cli && npx tsc --noEmit` must pass.
Owns ONLY .oh/cli/src/cli.ts. Does NOT create commands/update.ts (US-001), the test, probes, or docs.
userStories.notes: This branch is the SOLE overlap with open PR #334 (which adds an `init` branch in the same dispatch). At merge it is a trivial two-branch union — documented in the PR body.

userStories.title: Vitest suite .oh/cli/src/__tests__/update.test.ts
userStories.description: As CI's Test job, I need runUpdate's overlay, version gate, safety invariant, dry-run, and preconditions exhaustively covered.
acceptanceCriteria: Create NEW file .oh/cli/src/__tests__/update.test.ts using vitest (import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'). It is auto-discovered by vitest.config.ts's `.oh/cli/**/__tests__/**/*.test.ts` include. Import the unit under test as `import { runUpdate } from "../commands/update.js";`.

Build temp fixtures under a unique dir using node:os tmpdir + node:fs mkdtempSync (NOT Date/random in a way that breaks determinism — mkdtempSync is fine). Helper to create a fake equipped repo: <root>/.oh/cli/package.json with a given version + a couple of control-plane files (e.g. .oh/scripts/foo.sh, .oh/cli/src/cli.ts content), and a PROJECT file outside .oh/ (e.g. <root>/harness.yaml, <root>/src/app.ts) used to assert non-mutation. Collect IO via arrays: io = { stdout:(s)=>out.push(s), stderr:(s)=>err.push(s) }. afterEach rmSync(tmp, {recursive,force}).

Cover (each its own it()): (1) UPGRADE — from version 0.2.0 onto target 0.1.0 overlays a changed control-plane file and CREATES a new one; assert rc 0 and the file contents now match source. (2) PROJECT UNTOUCHED — after the same upgrade, the project file <root>/harness.yaml + <root>/src/app.ts are byte-identical to before (read before/after) and no file outside <root>/.oh/ was created. (3) EQUAL NO-OP — equal versions, no --force -> rc 0, stdout mentions 'already up to date', and NO target file changed. (4) FORCE re-overlay on equal versions -> rc 0 and files copied. (5) DOWNGRADE REFUSE — source 0.1.0 onto target 0.2.0, no force -> rc 1 + stderr mentions 'downgrade'; with force -> rc 0. PLUS a PRE-RELEASE case: source '0.1.0' vs target '0.1.0-dev' must be treated as EQUAL (no-op), proving the suffix-strip in the version parser (a naive parseInt would mis-rank). (6) DRY-RUN — upgrade with dryRun:true -> rc 0, every stdout line prefixed '[dry-run] ', and the target .oh/ is UNCHANGED on disk; PLUS force+dryRun on a downgrade writes NOTHING (dryRun suppresses writes even with --force). (7) PATH-ESCAPE — test the EXPORTED guard directly: `import { assertDestInTarget } from "../commands/update.js"`, then `expect(() => assertDestInTarget(path.resolve(targetOh, '../outside.ts'), targetOh, path.sep)).toThrow('refusing to write outside target .oh')` and `expect(() => assertDestInTarget(path.join(targetOh, 'cli/x.ts'), targetOh, path.sep)).not.toThrow()`. (A filesystem fixture cannot trigger it; the direct helper unit is the only reliable path.) Test (2)'s 'no file outside <root>/.oh/' assertion is the by-construction backstop. (8) PRECONDITIONS — missing --from source dir -> rc 1 + stderr 'update source not found'; target with no .oh/ -> rc 1 + stderr 'not an OpenHarness-equipped repo'; same-.oh source==target -> rc 1 + stderr 'same .oh'. (9) VOLATILE SKIP — a source file at a NESTED volatile segment, e.g. .oh/cli/node_modules/pkg/index.js AND .oh/cli/dist/oh.js, is NOT copied into target (proves the per-SEGMENT skip, not a top-level-only startsWith).

The whole `vitest run` (root config) stays green — your new file plus all existing suites. Run `/home/sandbox/harness/node_modules/.bin/vitest run --root <worktree>` (the worktree has no node_modules; resolution walks up to repo-root node_modules) to self-verify before reporting PASS.
Owns ONLY .oh/cli/src/__tests__/update.test.ts (new). Does NOT edit update.ts, cli.ts, or any other file.
userStories.notes: Use mkdtempSync for isolation; copy fixture file trees with explicit writeFileSync, not by reading the real repo .oh/.

userStories.title: Eval probe evals/probes/oh-update.sh + RESULTS row
userStories.description: As CI's eval gate, I need a 3-state probe that locks the `oh update` contract — the command exists, is .oh/-scoped, and is version-gated.
acceptanceCriteria: Create NEW evals/probes/oh-update.sh — 3-state oracle (exit 0 PASS / 1 REGRESSION / 2 SKIPPED). Header EXACTLY three comment lines: `# tier: A`, `# source: issue #531 Phase 3 (oh update — upgrade only the .oh control plane)`, `# desc: oh update refreshes ONLY the .oh/ control plane (path-escape-guarded) and is version-gated; project source stays untouched.`. `set -euo pipefail`. Resolve ROOT via `ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)"`.

SKIPPED(2, stderr 'SKIPPED oh update command not present') if .oh/cli/src/commands/update.ts is absent (lets the probe land green on bases without this slice). Otherwise PASS(0) requires ALL (each failure -> exit 1 with a one-line stderr 'REGRESSION <reason>'): update.ts exists and contains `export async function runUpdate`; update.ts contains the path-escape guard literal substring `refusing to write outside target .oh`; update.ts references `package.json` and a downgrade refusal (grep -q 'downgrade'); .oh/cli/src/cli.ts contains `runUpdate` AND a `first === "update"` dispatch (grep -Eq 'first === "update"'); cli.ts help advertises update (grep -q 'oh update'); the test file .oh/cli/src/__tests__/update.test.ts exists.

Negative guard (the core safety contract — a STATIC PROXY; say so in the `# desc:` so it is not mistaken for a runtime check): assert update.ts contains the exported guard by grepping ALL of `assertDestInTarget`, `targetOh`, and the message substring `refusing to write outside target .oh`. The real proof of the invariant is US-003's direct unit test of `assertDestInTarget` plus the project-untouched test; this probe only locks that the guard is not silently deleted.
shellcheck-clean (`shellcheck evals/probes/oh-update.sh` exits 0 at default level). Self-verify the probe exits 0 against the built worktree before reporting PASS.

Insert ONE new row into evals/RESULTS.md alphabetically (the table is sorted by probe id; `oh-update` sits after `oh-init-scaffold` if present, else in its alphabetical slot): `| oh-update | A | 2026-06-27 00:00 | PASS | issue #531 Phase 3 (oh update — upgrade only the .oh control plane) 2026-06-27 |`. Match the existing column shape exactly. Do NOT alter any other row.
Owns ONLY evals/probes/oh-update.sh (new) and evals/RESULTS.md. Does NOT touch the cli, tests, CHANGELOG, or README.
userStories.notes: If oh-init-scaffold.sh is absent on this base (it ships in #334), the alphabetical slot is still deterministic — place the row by simple ascii sort of the probe id column.

userStories.title: Doc the command: .oh/README.md + CHANGELOG
userStories.description: As OpenHarness, the new `oh update` command must be documented and the change recorded in the changelog.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit .oh/README.md: add a concise `## oh update (Phase 3)` (or fold into an existing CLI/commands section if one exists) documenting: purpose (upgrade ONLY the .oh/ control plane of an equipped repo; project source untouched); the safety invariant (writes only under <target>/.oh/, path-escape-guarded); usage `oh update --from <dir> [--dry-run] [--force]`; the `--from <built-checkout>` MVP and that remote-fetch is deferred (same precedent as `oh init`'s deferred bundling); the version gate (reuses .oh/cli/package.json#version — no separate VERSION file; refuses downgrade without --force, no-ops when already current). Disclose plainly (honesty): `oh update` OVERWRITES `.oh/` files in place with NO backup in this MVP, so any user-modified file UNDER `.oh/` (e.g. a local `.oh/config.json`) is replaced — only files OUTSIDE `.oh/` (the project) are guaranteed untouched. Explicitly contrast with `oh init` (init seeds PROJECT files from .oh/templates/; update refreshes the .oh/ infrastructure) so the two are not confused. Preserve the rest of the README.

Add a CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry (under ### Added) for `oh update`: 'Add `oh update` CLI command (RFC #531 Phase 3): upgrades only the .oh/ control plane of an OpenHarness-equipped repo from a `--from <checkout>` source, version-gated and path-escape-guarded so project source stays untouched; remote-fetch deferred.' Link the FULL URL https://github.com/mifunedev/openharness/issues/531 (NOT bare #531). If an [Unreleased] section/heading does not exist, add one at the top following the file's existing Keep-a-Changelog style.

Do NOT edit any code, test, probe, RESULTS.md, or cli file. Do NOT document remote-fetch as if implemented (it is deferred). Keep claims accurate: `oh update` requires a built source via --from in this slice.
Owns ONLY .oh/README.md and CHANGELOG.md. Does NOT touch anything else.
userStories.notes: If CHANGELOG already has an [Unreleased] block, append within it; do not create a duplicate.
